authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(149) — Stony Creek, and tributaries (Colusa, Glenn and Lake Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (149) Stony Creek, and tributaries (including the North, South, and Middle forks) from the headwaters downstream to the diversion dam west of Stonyford in the center of Section 35, T18N, R7W (Colusa, Glenn and Lake Cos.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: Upper Stony Creek Fishery Management Guidelines
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 267782, "Stony Creek", Stream, Colusa/Tehama/Glenn Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":267782,"gaz_name":"Stony Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Colusa"/"Tehama"/"Glenn"}; geometry multipoint, mouth point (Web Mercator -13577977.5144, 4819131.1625) converts to lon -121.973047, lat 39.677110, at the confluence with the Sacramento River near Hamilton City, Glenn Co. — corroborated by the Wikipedia infobox mouth coordinate for Stony Creek (Sacramento River), 39°40′38″N 121°58′23″W (39.67722°N, 121.97306°W). Corrects the file's previous coordinate (-122.6000, 39.3800), which was ~63 km southwest near the creek's headwaters/Stonyford area rather than its GNIS-designated point, and appears to have been a guessed estimate rather than a GNIS pull.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, catch-and-release only (0 trout bag), from the headwaters (incl. North, South, and Middle forks) downstream to the diversion dam west of Stonyford
regulations.verbatimText: Stony Creek, and tributaries (including the North, South, and Middle forks) from the headwaters downstream to the diversion dam west of Stonyford in the center of Section 35, T18N, R7W. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero trout may be retained; all trout caught must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit — catch and release only

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
